J1.basis: Diagnosed the site as **mid-pivot**... GSC data, which showed zero ranking presence for anything meeting/Lumina-related and all traffic sitting on legacy email-cleanup terms buried at position 40–60
J1.rationale: Explicitly names the pivot (live = Lumina meeting assistant, GSC = legacy email-cleanup demand) and frames the fixes around it; docked slightly because the GSC grounding is a loose aggregate ('position 40–60') rather than cited per-query figures.

J2.basis: Legacy InboxZap-era content. Kept live (they still hold indexed positions and impressions in Search Console) but the blog index must actually link to them
J2.rationale: Makes a single blanket 'keep all 12 live' disposition justified only by aggregate impressions — no asset-by-asset walk, no per-post impressions/positions, and no harvest/redirect/sunset differentiation; better than ignoring them but far short of a data-justified inventory.

J3.basis: The 12 blog posts themselves are 100% legacy InboxZap content... a content-strategy gap beyond this session's editable scope
J3.rationale: Avoids both failure modes — it neither torches legacy equity (fixes the orphaned links to preserve it) nor doubles down on inbox content — but it explicitly punts the meeting-assistant growth cluster and offers no sequenced transition, so the defensible middle path is only half-built.

J1.basis: Diagnosed the site as **mid-pivot**: content already reads "Lumina" (AI meeting assistant)... GSC data, which showed zero ranking presence for anything meeting/Lumina-related and all traffic sitting on legacy email-cleanup terms buried at position 40–60.
J1.rationale: Explicitly names the pivot, correctly reads live site = meeting assistant vs. GSC = legacy inbox demand vs. repo = partially stale, and frames every fix around that reality.

J2.basis: // Legacy InboxZap-era content. Kept live (they still hold indexed positions and impressions in Search Console) but the blog index must actually link to them
J2.rationale: Treats all 12 posts as one undifferentiated 'keep live' block with only a coarse position 40–60 reference — no asset-by-asset harvest/redirect/sunset dispositions grounded in actual per-post GSC impressions, closer to the blanket 'keep everything' anti-pattern.

J3.basis: The 12 blog posts themselves are 100% legacy InboxZap content... with no posts targeting meeting-assistant/transcription keywords — a content-strategy gap beyond this session's editable scope.
J3.rationale: Avoids both extremes — doesn't chase inbox content and doesn't torch legacy equity (restores crawlability instead) — but the growth side is only flagged as out-of-scope, with no sequenced transition or authority-bridging (e.g. redirecting salvageable posts toward the new cluster), so it lands a defensible but underdeveloped middle.

J1.basis: Diagnosed the site as **mid-pivot**... GSC data, which showed zero ranking presence for anything meeting/Lumina-related and all traffic sitting on legacy email-cleanup terms buried at position 40–60
J1.rationale: Explicitly names the pivot (live=Lumina meeting assistant, GSC=legacy email demand, repo partially stale) and frames the diagnosis around it; loses a point only because the framing serves a code-fix agenda rather than a market strategy.

J2.basis: Legacy InboxZap-era content. Kept live (they still hold indexed positions and impressions in Search Console) but the blog index must actually link to them
J2.rationale: Issues a single blanket 'keep all 12 live and link them' disposition with no per-asset harvest/301/sunset calls and no specific per-post impressions/positions cited; the one data reference is an aggregate ('position 40–60'), not the asset-by-asset grounding the question demands.

J3.basis: no posts targeting meeting-assistant/transcription keywords — a content-strategy gap beyond this session's editable scope
J3.rationale: Cleanly avoids both failure modes — preserves legacy equity via crawlable links/sitemap while flagging the missing meeting-assistant cluster — but stops at naming the gap; it neither builds the growth engine nor sequences the transition, leaving the middle path asserted rather than executable.
